title: Minority children at risk from ETS.
ehr_evidence: None; the study used blood cell biomarkers.
summary: A study evaluating the association between environmental tobacco smoke (ETS) exposure and cancer risk biomarkers in Hispanic and African-American children.
key_findings: Biomarkers increased with ETS exposure in all groups.
Young children are more vulnerable to genetic damage from carcinogens.
population: 109 Hispanic and African-American children, ages 1 to 6
